titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 560

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=830 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.412114+00 | translation moa=831 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.428846+00

div.p:
Edgarus dei gracia Rex Scotorum Omnibus ad quos presentes littere peruenerint tam Francis et Anglis quam Scotis . Salutem . Sciatis nos ex licencia
Willelmi regis Anglie superioris domini regni Scocie pro animabus Malcolmi patris nostri et Margarete matris nostre ac pro animabus edwardi et duncani fratrum nost
rorum necnon pro salute nostra dedisse et concessisse deo et sancto Cuthberto confessori Willelmo episcopo Drugeto priori et monachis dunolmie deo seruientibus
in perpetuum Baroniam et Manerium de Coldingham cum hiis mansionibus subscriptis scilicet Berwic Oldcambows Lummesden Reston’ Faudon’ Ayton’ alia Ayton’
Prendregest Cramesmouth Graiden Leinhale dilchestre Ederham Chirnside Hilton’ Blaketyr Hoton’ Raynton’ Paxton’ Fulden Morthington’
Lamberton’ alia lamberton’ Edington’ horford Fishewic et vpesetlington cum omnibus pertinenciis iuribus et decimis garbarum et feni cum omnibus libertatibus
et franchesiis regalibus adeo libere et plenarie sicut predictus pater noster et dominus illa tenuit . Habenda et tenenda omnia et singula predicta Baroniam et Mane
rium mansiones decimas cum omnibus libertatibus Franchesiis regalibus et iuribus quibuscumque predictis episcopo priori et monachis ac eorum successoribus
de nobis et heredibus nostris in puram et perpetuam elemosinam in perpetuum . Et nos et heredes nostri omnia et singula predicta Baroniam Manerium mansiones
decimas cum ominbus libertatibus Franchesiis Regalibus et iuribus quibuscumque ut predictum est prefatis episcopo priori et monachis et eorum successoribus contra
omnes gentes protegemus imperpetuum et defendemus . In cuius rei testimonium presentibus litteris nostris sigillum nostrum apponi fecimus apud Norham in presencia
predicti Regis Willelmi Anglie superioris domini regni Scocie et predictorum episcopi et prioris Walteri Valonis Odnelli Umfrauile Rachonis de Loreyns Odnelli
Heron Roberti de Amundi villa et maximae multitudinis Francorum Anglorum et Scotorum

p.cl: Edgar by the grace of God king of the Scots to all to whom the present letters shall have come, as much to Frenchmen and English as Gaelic, greeting.

us, from the licence of William the king of England, overlord of the kingdom of Scotland, for the sake of the souls of Malcolm our father and Margaret our mother, and for the souls of Edward and Duncan our brothers, and also for the sake of our own soul, to have given and conceded to God and Saint Cuthbert the confessor, to William the bishop, and Turgot the prior, and to the monks of Durham serving God in perpetuity, the barony and manor of Coldingham, with these manors written below, to wit, Berwick, Old Cambus, Lumsdaine, Reston, 'Faudon', Ayton, the other Ayton, Prederguest, Cramsmoo, Graden, Lennel, 'Dilchestre', Edrom, Chirnside, Hilton, Blackadder, Hutton, Renton, Paxton, 'Fulden', Mordington, Lamberton, the other Lamberton, Edrington, Horford, and Upsettlington, with all pertinents, rights, and teinds of wheat and grain crops and hay, with all liberties and royal immunities as freely and fully as our aforesaid father and lord held it. For the aforesaid bishop, prior, and monks and their successors to have and to hold barony and manor, dwellings and teinds, all in general and in particular, with all liberties, royal immunities, and rights whatsoever, of us and our heirs in pure and perpetual alms for ever. And we and our heirs shall protect and defend for the bishop, prior, monks, and their successors, the aforesaid barony and manor, dwellings and teinds, all in general and particular, with all liberties, royal immunities and rights whatsoever, as it has been said before, against all people.
In testimony of this this matter we have caused our seal to be applied to the present letters at Norham

in the presence of the aforesaid King William of England the overlord of the kingdom of Scotland, and of the aforesaid bishop and prior, of Walter de Valognes, Odinel de Umfraville, Racho de Loreyns, Odinel Heron, Robert de Mandeville, and a great multitude of French, English and Scots.
